untranslatable_reason: This signal requires comparative analysis across a portfolio universe (identifying N best and N worst performers in a calendar month relative to the same month one year prior), then executing paired long/short positions based on that ranking. The allowed indicators are limited to single-instrument technical metrics (RSI, MACD, moving averages, etc.) and cannot express: (1) cross-sectional ranking logic (top N vs bottom N of a universe), (2) year-over-year return comparison across multiple assets, (3) portfolio-level rebalancing at specific calendar dates, or (4) dynamic universe selection based on historical performance ranking. This is a factor/pattern-based statistical arbitrage strategy that requires portfolio construction logic outside the scope of single-instrument indicator conditions.
translation_notes: Signal CHAN_QT_DRY_0 is a seasonal mean-reversion/momentum reversal strategy operating at the portfolio level with year-on-year calendar month seasonality. It cannot be reduced to indicator crossovers or thresholds on a single security. Implementation would require a separate portfolio ranking and position-sizing module. The 'effect has disappeared since 2002' notation suggests this pattern no longer has statistical validity in modern markets.
